REPLY TO SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS'S MOTION TO QUASH FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION FILED BY DEFENDANT HARRAH'S ENTERTAINMENT, INC., A CORPORATION HARRAH'S OPERATING COMPANY, INC., A CORPORATION HARVEYS TAHOE MANAGEMENT COMPANY, INC., A CORPORATION GIVAN, BARRY ,AN INDIVIDUAL HARVEY'S LAKE TAHOE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HARRAH'S ENTERTAINMENT, INC., HARRAH'S OPERATING COMPANY, INC., HARVEYS TAHOE MANAGEMENT COMPANY, INC. AND HARVEY'S LAKE TAHOE MOTION TO QUASH FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. ALTHOUGH THERE DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE GENERAL JURISDICTION OVER THESE DEFENDANTS, THEY ARE SUBJECT TO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANTS PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED THEMSELVES OF THE BENEFITS AND PRIVILEGES OF CONDUCTING BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA THROUGH THEIR VARIOUS METHODS OF ADVERTISING CORRESPONDENCE AND INTERACTIVE WEBSITE. A SUBSTANTIAL CONNECTION EXISTS BETWEEN PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM AND DEFENDANTS' ACTIVITIES THAT ENTICED PLAINTIFF TO VISIT HARVEY'S WHERE SHE WAS INJURED. THE EXERCISE OF JURISDICTION OVER THESE DEFENDANTS COMPORTS WITH TRADITIONAL MOTIONS OF FAIR PLAY AND SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE AND IS REASONABLE. GIVEN THE DEPTH OF SOLICITATION OF CALIFORNIA PATRONAGE UNDERTAKEN BY THESE DEFENDANTS, IT IS REASONABLE THAT THEY WOULD FORESEE THAT THEY WOULD BE SUBJECTED TO CALIFORNIA JURISDICTION. SEE SNOWNEY VS. HARRAH'S ENTERTAINMENT, INC. (2005) 35 CAL. 4TH 1054. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BARRY GIVAN MOTION TO QUASH FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. GIVAN IS NOT SUBJECT TO GENERAL JURISDICTION BECAUSE HE LACKS SUBSTANTIAL, SYSTEMATIC, AND CONTINUOUS CONTACT WITH CALIFORNIA. GIVAN IS NOT SUBJECT TO SPECIFIC JURISDICTION. HE DID NOT PURPOSEFULLY AVAIL HIMSELF OF THE BENEFITS AND PRIVILEGES OF CONDUCTING BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA, WAS EMPLOYED IN NEVADA, AND SERVED CUSTOMERS WHO COME TO HIM OF THEIR OWN ACCORD. THERE IS NOT SUBSTANTIAL NEXUS BETWEEN GIVAN'S MINIMAL CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA AND THE ALLEGED INCIDENT. IT WOULD BE UNFAIR AND UNREASONABLE TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER GIVAN IN CALIFORNIA. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HARRAH'S ENTERTAINMENT, INC., HARRAH'S OPERATING COMPANY, INC., HARVEYS TAHOE MANAGEMENT COMPANY, INC., and, HARVEY'S LAKE TAHOE Motion to Quash for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction. Appearances: Loren E. Schwartz, Esq. & Christopher Dolan, Esq. The Dolan Law Firm, (415) 421-2800, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Arnold Laub, Law Offices of Arnold Laub (415) 362-0101, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Robert H. Zimmerman, Esq. of Schuering Zimmerman & Doyle LLP (916) 567-0400 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Although there does not appear to be general jurisdiction over these defendants, they are subject to specific jurisdiction in California. Defendants purposefully availed themselves of the benefits and privileges of conducting business in California through their various methods of advertising correspondence and interactive website. A substantial connection exists between plaintiff's claim and defendants' activities that enticed plaintiff to visit Harvey's where she was injured. The exercise of jurisdiction over these defendants comports with traditional motions of fair play and substantial justice and is reasonable. Given the depth of solicitation of California patronage undertaken by these defendants, it is reasonable that they would foresee that they would be subjected to California jurisdiction. See Snowney vs. Harrah's Entertainment, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1054. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BARRY GIVAN Motion to Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Appearances: Loren E. Schwartz, Esq. & Christopher Dolan, Esq. The Dolan Law Firm, (415) 421-2800, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Arnold Laub, Law Offices of Arnold Laub (415) 362-0101, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Robert H. Zimmerman, Esq. of Schuering Zimmerman & Doyle LLP (916) 567-0400 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. Givan is not subject to general jurisdiction because he lacks substantial, systematic, and continuous contact with California. Givan is not subject to specific jurisdiction. He did not purposefully avail himself of the benefits and privileges of conducting business in California, was employed in Nevada, and served customers who come to him of their own accord. There is not substantial nexus between Givan's minimal contacts with California and the alleged incident. It would be unfair and unreasonable to exercise jurisdiction over Givan in California. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
